question: Which two Texas counties were in the top five for SSO LunchTotal in every program year across the four program years starting from the year (within 2011-2015) that has the lowest birth rate for unmarried females ages 15-19 in the United States and also had the largest percentage decreases in day care home meal reimbursements between 2018-2019 and 2020-2021? List the county names (no 'County' suffix) in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Filtering to Age Group = "15-19 years" and Race or Hispanic Origin = "All Races" for 2011-2015, the minimum Birth Rate is 20.2 in 2015.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2015 has the lowest birth rate for unmarried females ages 15-19 in the United States? (Filter Age Group == "15-19 years" and Race or Hispanic Origin == "All Races"; select the minimum Birth Rate.)

5.fact: In the <node_1 answer + 3>-<node_1 answer + 4> SSO meal count program period dataset, the top five counties by LunchTotal were Hidalgo (668,927), Harris (525,238), Dallas (348,923), Bexar (284,028), and Cameron (260,791).

6.subquestion: Among {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron} (from hop 1), which counties had the largest percentage decrease in CACFP day care home meal reimbursements between the 2018-2019 and 2020-2021 program years?
